canto_title: The Descent into the First Bolgia of Malebolge

scenes.summary: Dante describes the structure of the ditches and the rocky walls of the eighth circle of Hell.
The poets arrive in the first ditch, which is filled with sinners being beaten by tormentors.
Dante observes the continuous, massive stream of souls moving across the bridge like a crowd during a jubilee.
Horned demons cruelly lash the sinners, who endure the blows without even expecting the next.
Dante recognizes a specific Venetian sinner and confronts him about his identity.
The sinner explains his identity and recounts his role in a scandalous historical affair.
A demon strikes the sinner and silences him, telling him there are no women to be found here.
Dante and Virgil catch up to their companions and climb up a ledge of the ditch.
Virgil instructs Dante to observe the other newly arrived sinners whose faces they have not yet seen.
The poets watch the traffic of souls crossing the bridge from the opposite side.
Virgil points out Jason, the leader of the Argonauts, and describes his betrayal of Medea and Isifile.
The poets reach a crossroads characterized by a nauseating, stagnant atmosphere and a thick, foul odor.
Dante spots a man submerged in a pit of excrement.
Dante recognizes the man as Alessio Interminei, a notorious flatterer from Lucca.
The sinner admits that he was undone by his own addiction to smooth-talking and flattery.
Virgil directs Dante's attention to a woman covered in filth, the prostitute Taïde, to complete their visual tour.
